name: OrtoSat 30 cm - Portugal Continental – 2023
description: 3. Data Description: The OrtoSat2023 FrameworkThe OrtoSat2023 coverage is a product of the Land Occupation Monitoring System (Sistema de Monitorização da Ocupação do Solo - SMOS), an initiative designed and developed by the Directorate-General for Territory (DGT) to continuously produce cartographic information regarding national land use and land cover. SMOS leverages state-of-the-art space technologies and Artificial Intelligence to deliver high-resolution, rapid-turnaround cartographic products under an open data policy. All products can be visualized via the viSMOS platform (https://smos.dgterritorio.gov.pt/vi-smos).3.1 Platform Characteristics and Radiometric SpecificationsThe OrtoSat2023 layer consists of orthoimages captured by very high spatial resolution satellites during the year 2023 across mainland Portugal. The dataset forms an equalized, seamless mosaic of orthorectified satellite imagery with a spatial resolution (Ground Sampling Distance - GSD) of $30\text{ cm}$. The imagery used to construct the mosaic was acquired by the Pleiades-Neo (3 & 4) satellite constellation between April and October 2023.This image coverage was funded by Investment RE-CCT-C8-i2 (Rustic Property Cadastre and Land Occupation Monitoring System) under the Portuguese Recovery and Resilience Plan (PRR), and produced via an international public tender (CP/154/2023) implemented by Inforgeo—the national representative of Airbus, the owner of the Pleiades-Neo constellation.The final mosaic integrates multiple images captured on different dates within the April–October window. Global consistency is maintained via radiometric block adjustments, contrast balancing, and the strategic placement of seamlines between orthorectified images to maximize cloud, haze, and smoke-free coverage.The dataset features an 8-bit radiometric resolution (256 digital numbers) across four available spectral bands:Red: $620 \text{ to } 690 \text{ nm}$Green: $530 \text{ to } 590 \text{ nm}$Blue: $450 \text{ to } 520 \text{ nm}$Near-Infrared (NIR): $770 \text{ to } 880 \text{ nm}$Atmospheric and geometric corrections were applied directly to the raw imagery. The mosaic equalization pipeline incorporated pansharpening algorithms alongside radiometric block adjustments. Orthorectification was executed utilizing the Airbus WorldDEM4Ortho digital elevation model, which features an approximate spatial resolution of $24\text{ m}$ and a global vertical accuracy of $4\text{ m}$. Image orientation was further enhanced using an Airbus Space Reference Points (SRP) ground control database.3.2 Positional Accuracy and Technical ValidationThe positional accuracy of OrtoSat2023 was validated using independent ground control points, yielding a Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of $2.2\text{ m}$ and a maximum planimetric displacement of $4.7\text{ m}$. These results strictly satisfy the product specifications (target $\text{RMSE} < 3.5\text{ m}$ and $\text{maximum displacement} \le 5\text{ m}$).Minor localized errors remain present—as is standard for this class of wide-area satellite mosaic—though they do not compromise analytical integrity. These include minor positional shifts or localized blurring along seamlines, geometric distortions on bridges or overpasses, slight radiometric discrepancies across seamlines, and isolated occurrences of residual clouds, haze, or cloud shadows.3.3 Data Distribution and Access PoliciesThe spatial coverage for mainland Portugal was released in phases corresponding to contractual milestones:Phase 1 (July 2024): A central region swath covering approximately $40\%$ of the territory.Phase 2 (September 2024): The remaining $60\%$ of the territory, encompassing the Northern and Southern regions.The imagery is distributed in two distinct color composites:True Color ("CorVerdadeira"): RGB configuration mapping Red, Green, and Blue bands.False Color ("FalsaCor"): RGB configuration mapping Near-Infrared, Red, and Green bands.The dataset is partitioned using a standardized grid indexing system measuring approximately $4\text{ km} \times 5\text{ km}$ in the East-West and North-South directions, resulting in a total of 4,884 map sheets (sections). Access to OrtoSat2023 is split into two modalities:a) Visualization Service: Publicly accessible via an Open Geospatial Consortium (OGC) compliant WMS service, displaying both the True Color and False Color composites.b) Download Service: Restricted exclusively to Public Administration entities due to licensing conditions. Eligible entities must sign a Terms of Use agreement (available at https://smos.dgterritorio.gov.pt/cartografia-de-base). Access credentials are provided individually via MS Sharepoint.True Color composites can be downloaded by original grid section, or clipped into single consolidated files by Municipality, NUTS III, or NUTS II boundaries. False Color composites are strictly downloadable by individual grid sections. Each delivered file consists of 3 spectral bands and is formatted using the OGC Cloud Optimized GeoTIFF (COG) standard.The original, unrectified raw satellite imagery used to compile the mosaic can be made available upon formal request for specialized digital image processing applications. Guidance on consuming these datasets via OGC APIs, WMS, WMTS, or WFS can be found on the DGT Open Data support portal (https://www.dgterritorio.gov.pt/dados-abertos).
time_steps_other: there is no predefined interval. data is from 2023
access_barrier: No, direct download/access through a web link
